channel.title: Vegan Home Cooks
channel.description: Recent content on Vegan Home Cooks

item.title: Deli Slice Seitan
item.description:
image: Deli slice day. Ham, herbed turkey, turkey w Montreal steak spice. Letting em all cool before refrigerating overnight and slicing tomorrow

seitan recipe for @skrimem (for ham specifically - omit tomato paste, dark soy sauce, maple syrup, and paprika for more of a turkey, in which case i’d add poultry spice)

  • 1 block extra firm tofu, drained (i buy 350g packs; use whatever’s in there instead of worrying about weighing it outside of the package)
  • 2Tbsp tapioca starch (or 1T cornstarch)
  • 30g nutritional yeast
  • 1/2Tbsp garlic powder
  • 1/2Tbsp onion powder
  • 2tsp smoked paprika
  • 1tsp liquid smoke
  • 1/2tsp baking powder
  • 2tsp msg (optional)
  • 1tsp dark soy sauce
  • 1tsp tomato paste
  • 1tsp maple syrup
  • 1Tbsp bouillon (i use veg beef bouillon, swap to regular veg or veg chicken for turkey slices)
  • 2Tbsp olive oil
  • couple splashes of water (see instructions)
  • APPROX 180g vital wheat gluten (see instructions)
  1. add all ingredients except for the vital wheat gluten water into a large food processor, and process until smooth
  2. add water, a tablespoon at a time, until the mixture reaches a hummus-like consistency
  3. add the vital wheat gluten and process on high. you may want to remove half the mixture first before adding the vwg if your food processor is on the smaller side. you may need a little more or a little less vwg - horrifically, i eyeball this so my measurement is a guess, but your final “dough” should be firm, uniform, and not dry
  4. process until the the consistency goes from small crumbly bits to a more uniform dough
  5. turn out onto a clean counter and knead a couple of times to work out any bumpy bits.
  6. shape the dough into two logs with your hands
  7. place onto a piece of parchment paper large enough to wrap around the entire log. if you’d like, you can add a little more oil here and add a rub - for ham i’d recommend dijon and maple syrup. for turkey i like to add dried sage and/or thyme with black pepper. distribute the rub well, wrap in the paper, and finally wrap tightly with foil.
  8. preheat the oven to 350. allow the dough to rest while the oven preheats
  9. bake for 1hr, flipping after 30 min 10.allow to cool for at least 30 min without unwrapping. then, unwrap and allow to cool to room temperature before storing in an airtight container
  10. the next day, after a night in the fridge, slice or cut into the shapes you want

btw - this (for me) makes 2-3 logs of seitan depending on how much you would like of each variety, if you decide to do more than one. even if you want it all the same flavour i’d still recommend breaking it into two equally-sized logs - 1hr in the oven will be enough for 2 or 3 logs, but not enough for 1

item.title: Okara Nuggets
item.description:
Measurements will be added next time these are made. Here’s a quick summary for now.

Ingredients

  • Wet okara (cooked okara works well, but raw is fine too since these get cooked a lot — if your okara is dry, add any liquid like plant milk, broth, tomato product, etc.)
  • Vital wheat gluten (VWG)
  • Flour
  • Chick’n bouillon (or any bouillon, or whatever spices you want)

Instructions

Make the dough

Use a food processor to blend for 30 seconds or until well combined. The dough should clump up and come away from the walls of the food processor, like seitan.

item.title: Lentil Tempeh
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups dry brown lentils
  • 1.7-ish liters water (for boiling)
  • 2 tbsp white vinegar
  • 2.5 tsp tempeh starter (Raprima bulk starter — store in the fridge)

Equipment

  • 2 sandwich bags
  • Toothpick (for poking holes in bags)
  • Small kitchen scale
  • Oven with a functioning light (acts as incubator)
  • Large mixing bowl
  • Kitchen towel (for cooling lentils)
  • Strainer
  • Racks
  • Thermometer

Instructions

Step 1 — Cook the lentils

Rinse and cook lentils for 12-13 minutes but NO MORE. Meanwhile poke lots of holes in the sandwich bags w the toothpick.

item.title: Javant's Apple Cakes
item.description:
Ingredients

Cake

  • 1 cup oat flour (for nut free use 1 ¼ cup oat flour and omit the almond flour)
  • ⅓ cup almond flour
  • 1 1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract)
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 4-6 pitted Medjool dates
  • 1/3 cup plant milk
  • ⅓ cup apple sauce
  • 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
  • 2 tbsp cashew butter (Nut Free: Use tahini)

Apple Pie Filling

  • 2 apples, diced small
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract)
  • ½ cup water
  • 2 tsp lemon juice

Date Caramel

  • 6 pitted medjool dates
  • ⅓ cup water or plant milk
  • ½ tsp vanilla bean powder (or extract)

Instructions

Preheat the oven to 350F.

item.title: Novick Burger Formula
item.description:
If you prefer a write up: Brand New Vegan - Jeff Novick Bean Burgers

The Formula

  • 14 oz can no-salt beans
  • half cup rolled oats
  • half cup cooked brown rice (or any starch)
  • 2 tbsp tomato product (tomato sauce, paste, crushed tomatoes etc)
  • 2 tbsp mixed spice (be creative!)

mash them all up and bake!

The VHC Theme Song Burgers in Paradise

item.title: Strawberry Love Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1.5 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1/2 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup unsweetened plant milk
  • 1/4 cup lemon juice
  • 1/2 cup olive oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 cup fresh strawberries, chopped

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375°F / 190°C
  2. Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl
  3. Mix wet ingredients separately then fold into dry
  4. Fold in strawberries
  5. Fill muffin tin 3/4 full
  6. Bake 20-25 minutes until golden

item.title: Hamid's Pancakes
item.description:
Original recipe

  • I have 3 scoops, 1 large “half cup”, 1 medium “2 tbsp” and 1 small “half tsp”

  • dry: 1.5 cups ww flour, .5 tsp baking soda, 1.5 tsp baking powder, .25 tsp salt

  • wet: 2 tbsp flax meal mixed with 6 tbsp water + 1.5 cups soy milk mixed with 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar and 2 tbsp maple syrup or molasses + one mashed banana

  1. Mix dry, mix flax egg, mix milk, mix egg and milk, mash the banana and add some of the milk to it to loosen, add the wet into the flour and stir, add the banana and fold in
